description:
The message list does not maintain scroll lock during streaming. New tokens cause layout shifts without compensating scroll, so the user loses the tail of the message. No detection of intentional scroll-up to release the lock.

**Fix:** Create a `useScrollLock` hook. Lock acquired on new stream start. `useLayoutEffect` fires synchronously after DOM mutations to scroll to `scrollHeight - clientHeight` before paint (prevents the 1-frame lag of `useEffect`). User scroll-up >50px from bottom releases lock. Next new message re-acquires.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/ava/src/hooks/useScrollLock.ts` — create: the hook
- `apps/ava/src/components/chat/MessageList.tsx` — apply useScrollLock, pass container ref
- `apps/ava/src/components/chat/ChatPane.tsx` — ensure container ref is accessible

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Viewport stays pinned to bottom throughout a 500-token streamed message (no drift)
- Scroll up mid-stream → lock releases → stream continues → viewport does not jump
- New message after user scrolls up → viewport snaps back, lock re-acquires
- `useLayoutEffect` used (not `useEffect`) — enforced in PR review
- Build passes, all tests pass

**Complexity:** medium
title: Scroll lock with useLayoutEffect for streaming messages
